validation_gates.reason: Verified repository identity, default-branch base c8427d30484ee227be485f925019ebc48f08bc1d, current bug.md stub and blob, issue-intake governance, security routing, CODEOWNERS ownership, receipt schema, and absence of overlapping open bug-template work.

Recovered prior repository bug-template material covering reproducibility, environment, geospatial context, evidence IDs, logs, impact, safe redaction, validation, and correction posture; retained and modernized those useful obligations without treating historical paths as current facts.

Parsed front matter with name/about/title/labels/assignees, verified balanced HTML comments and code fences, final newline, ordered sections, no unverified labels, and assignment to verified repository owner bartytime4life.

The template routes security-sensitive reports to SECURITY.md, prohibits restricted public payloads, distinguishes allegation from confirmation, and states that filing, assignment, automation, and issue closure do not grant evidence, correction, release, or publication authority.

The final template requires repository context, minimal public-safe reproduction, expected versus actual behavior, regression history, EvidenceRef/EvidenceBundle posture, temporal/spatial scope, trust impact, prior validation, regression tests, acceptance criteria, and rollback/correction linkage.
Computed SHA-256 6e54e9cacf63aab46db2c5b7c54a986d2ea8ae7c444925d4bd3420282dece102 and Git blob SHA 61d1554c1c014ed369ab4c00c3b06373bd93d8e8; GitHub returned the same blob SHA after the update.
The template is not yet on the default branch. Issue chooser rendering, repository issue settings, automation, and live required-review behavior remain NEEDS VERIFICATION after merge.

notes: AI-authored replacement of the public bug issue-template stub on a scoped review branch. The artifact structures intake and triage only. It does not confirm a defect, create evidence authority, authorize testing, approve remediation, change policy, approve correction or release, or grant publication authority. Historical bug-template commits were used as evidence for valuable prior requirements, not as current implementation proof. Human review remains pending.
